normalized_definitions.experimental_test_summary: A high-level overview of the experimental procedure, detailing the test type, electrochemical modes, solution chemistry, operating parameters, and overarching environmental conditions.
normalized_definitions.pitting_potential: The explicitly measured critical potential at which stable, localized pitting initiation occurs on the material surface during an anodic polarization sweep.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_temperature: The measured and controlled thermodynamic temperature of the bulk test solution or electrolyte, which strongly influences thermodynamic stability and localized corrosion susceptibility.
normalized_definitions.polarization_scan_rate: The continuous potential sweep rate or the discrete step-size and holding duration applied to change the electrical potential during potentiodynamic polarization testing.
normalized_definitions.reference_electrode: The specific standard reference electrode (e.g., SCE, Ag/AgCl) used during electrochemical testing to measure, reference, and standardize electrical potentials.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_composition: The precise chemical formulation of the testing solution, including solvents, molar concentrations of primary salts, and the exact identity and concentration of any additives or soluble inhibitors.
normalized_definitions.aeration_status: The dissolved gas condition within the bulk electrolyte, denoting whether the solution was actively aerated, naturally exposed to air, or deaerated/purged with an inert gas.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_potential: The measured open-circuit resting potential (Ecorr) of the working electrode at steady state in the test solution, serving as the thermodynamic baseline prior to external polarization.
normalized_definitions.ocp_stabilization_protocol: The specified rest period, defined time duration, or fluctuation threshold applied to ensure the working electrode reaches a steady-state open-circuit potential equilibrium prior to active testing.
normalized_definitions.counter_electrode_details: The material composition, structural form, physical geometry, exposed surface area, and isolation method of the auxiliary or counter electrode used to complete the electrical circuit.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_ph: The explicitly measured, controlled, or actively adjusted hydrogen ion concentration (pH) of the bulk testing electrolyte, serving as a critical standalone thermodynamic variable governing passive film stability and pitting potential independent of general salt composition.
normalized_definitions.repassivation_potential: The measured critical protection potential (Ep or Erp) during a reverse potentiodynamic polarization scan at which actively growing localized pits repassivate, active dissolution ceases, and the anodic current drops back to passive levels.
normalized_definitions.passive_current_density: The measured baseline or steady-state anodic current density (ipassive) maintained by the protective oxide film across the passive potential region prior to localized pitting or transpassive breakdown.
normalized_definitions.pitting_breakdown_criterion: The explicit quantitative mathematical or visual threshold (e.g., a specific anodic current density limit) used objectively by researchers to define and record the exact onset of the critical pitting potential.
normalized_definitions.scan_reversal_criterion: The predefined experimental threshold (such as a specific current limit, current density, or maximum vertex potential) reached during the forward anodic scan that triggers the reversal of the polarization sweep to evaluate repassivation kinetics and pit growth stability.
normalized_definitions.hysteresis_charge_flow: The integrated electric charge representing the area within the hysteresis loop during a reversed cyclic polarization scan, used to quantify the extent of localized corrosion propagation.
normalized_definitions.electrochemical_instrumentation: The specific make, model, and hardware/software configuration of the potentiostat or equipment used to conduct electrochemical tests and calculate polarization parameters.
normalized_definitions.electrochemical_cell_type: The physical hardware configuration or design of the testing container (e.g., conventional flat cell, micro-capillary cell) used to interface the working electrode with the electrolyte, influencing measurement geometries.
normalized_definitions.transpassive_potential: The critical potential (Etrans) at which the passive film breaks down uniformly due to bulk thermodynamic instability or oxidation of key alloying elements into higher-valency soluble states, establishing the upper limit for passivity.
normalized_definitions.passive_film_impedance: The measured alternating current (AC) impedance or direct current (DC) resistance limit of the passive film at a specific hold potential, quantitatively indicating the film's protective quality and resistance to ionic transport.
normalized_definitions.hydrodynamic_conditions: The controlled fluid flow, stirring mechanism, or specific specimen rotation rate (e.g., in rpm) applied to quantitatively control hydrodynamic mass transport and diffusion layer conditions at the electrode-electrolyte interface.
normalized_definitions.cathodic_preconditioning_protocol: The deliberate in-situ application of a specific cathodic potential or current for a defined duration prior to the main test, designed to electrochemically reduce native air-formed oxides and establish a reproducible standard surface state.
normalized_definitions.artificial_pit_depth: The specific depth to which pits are intentionally electrochemically grown or pre-dissolved prior to testing in order to study repassivation characteristics dependent on pit depth.
normalized_definitions.primary_passivation_potential: The measured electrical potential (Epp or Ekp) during an anodic sweep at which the material transitions from an active dissolution state to a protective passive state, typically characterized by a peak active current.
normalized_definitions.critical_passivation_current_density: The maximum or peak active anodic current density (Icrit or ikp) observed at the primary passivation potential immediately prior to the formation of the protective passive film.
normalized_definitions.potentiostatic_holding_potential: The explicit, constant anodic electrical potential applied to the working electrode to maintain the alloy in a targeted passive state while monitoring for delayed passive film breakdown.
normalized_definitions.dynamic_electrolyte_alteration: The precise, in-situ timed addition of aggressive species or controlled changes to the solution chemistry while the electrochemical test is actively running.
normalized_definitions.time_to_pitting_breakdown: The measured duration of time elapsed under a constant applied potential, or following a dynamic electrolyte alteration, before a permanent, logarithmic increase in anodic current signals stable pit initiation.
